title: Butter, Oils, and Fats
content:
BUTTER

Must contain 80% fat by law. Sweet butter (unsalted) preferred for cooking. Salt butter keeps longer. Store covered in refrigerator up to 2 weeks; freeze up to 6 months. One pound = 2 cups = 4 sticks (½ cup each).

To Clarify: Melt slowly; skim; allow to cool; remove fat cake. Use for sealing potted meats or slower browning.

Substitution: Butter for shortening = measure for measure. For oil = reduce 15-20%.

MARGARINE

Must contain 80% fat. Usually emulsion of milk and vegetable oils. May be substituted weight-for-weight or measure-for-measure for butter, though flavor differs. Keep refrigerated, tightly covered.

VEGETABLE SHORTENINGS

Hydrogenated vegetable oils (soybean, corn, cottonseed). Often contain emulsifiers and air for baking volume. Bland flavor; store at 70° in covered tin.

Substitution for butter: measure for measure (water in butter compensates for air in shortening). By weight: use 15-20% less shortening than butter.

OILS

Sources: corn, cottonseed, soybean, safflower, peanut, olive, sesame. Cold-pressed retains more nutrients. Safflower, soybean, cottonseed have higher smoking points than peanut or sesame. Olive oil lowest (around 400°F).

Substitution for butter: reduce 15-20% by measure or weight. Not ideal for all baking—use recipes specifically developed for oil.
